commit.message:
Merge metadata and captions into unified data/ parquets

Replace separate captions/ and metadata/ parquet directories with a
single data/ directory containing unified parquets with all columns:
image_id, width, height, aspect_bucket, caption.

HF data viewer now shows one clean 70k-row table instead of 140k
rows from two separate parquet sets.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: stratum-hq
repository.description: Stratum-HQ is a high-fidelity human dataset enrichment engine. It transforms raw images into dense, multi-layered training data by integrating T5-Large text embeddings, DINOv3 visual and spatial tokens, and 133-point DWPose keypoints. Optimized for Sapiens and consumer GPUs, it bridges the gap between pixels and anatomical structure.

commit.message:
Add HF dataset configs for captions and metadata subsets

Declares named configs in the dataset card YAML front matter so
HuggingFace treats captions/ and metadata/ parquets as separate
subsets instead of concatenating all rows (70k + 70k = 140k).

Users load with: load_dataset('216pi/stratum-ffhq', 'captions')

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Use range-labeled parquet paths for captions and metadata

Captions and metadata parquets now use the same range-labeled naming
as npy tar files (e.g. captions/00000-09999.parquet instead of
captions/captions.parquet). This makes incremental --offset/--limit
publishing additive rather than overwriting previous batches.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: stratum-hq
repository.description: Stratum-HQ is a high-fidelity human dataset enrichment engine. It transforms raw images into dense, multi-layered training data by integrating T5-Large text embeddings, DINOv3 visual and spatial tokens, and 133-point DWPose keypoints. Optimized for Sapiens and consumer GPUs, it bridges the gap between pixels and anatomical structure.

commit.message:
Fix DINOv3 register token ordering in pipeline and visualization

DINOv3 token sequence is [CLS, reg1..reg4, patch1..patchN], not
[CLS, patch1..patchN, reg1..reg4] as previously assumed.

Pipeline: slice from index 5 onward to get only spatial patches.
Visualization: auto-detect and strip leading register tokens in
existing data (first 4 have ~1.6× higher norm than spatial patches).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: Stratum-HQ is a high-fidelity human dataset enrichment engine. commit.message:
Fix DINOv3 heatmap spatial alignment

Replace BILINEAR upscale with block repetition (np.repeat) so each
patch maps exactly to its 16×16 pixel region without interpolation
offset.

Fix visualizations and add per-image attribution

- Caption panel: dynamic height to fit full text (no more cut-off)
- DINOv3 heatmap: percentile normalization (2nd-98th) + 60% blend for
  much stronger contrast

  flat binary mask; viridis coloring + red padding boundary marker
- examples/CREDITS.md: per-image Flickr author, license, and URL for all
  3 example photos (CC BY-NC 2.0 / CC BY 2.0)

commit.message:
Move config.yaml out of version control

Bundle a default config as loki/config.default.yaml inside the package.
On `loki setup`, copy it to the project root only if config.yaml does not
already exist, so user edits are never overwritten. The existing config
review flow (display + confirm) is unchanged regardless of whether the
file was just seeded from defaults or already present.

- Add loki/config.default.yaml (bundled defaults, committed)
- Remove config.yaml from git tracking (git rm --cached); add to .gitignore
- setup(): copy bundled default before load_config() when file is absent
- load_config(): improve missing-file error to point users to `loki setup`
- tests: add copy-on-missing and no-overwrite-on-existing test cases

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Use hardlinks for npy migration with copy fallback

Hardlinks avoid doubling ~280 GB of disk usage when source and destination
are on the same filesystem. Falls back to shutil.copy2() for cross-filesystem
migrations.

Tested: 69,995 FFHQ images migrated in ~12 min (vs ~30 hr for full copy).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add migrate command for converting prx-tg per-modality format to stratum per-image format

Adds 'stratum migrate' subcommand that reads the old approved_image_dataset.jsonl
and per-modality npy directories (dinov3/, t5_hidden/, etc.) and creates stratum
per-image directories with properly renamed artifacts.

Features:
- Copies npy files with correct naming (dinov3/{id}.npy -> {id}/dinov3_cls.npy, etc.)
- Converts JSONL t5_attention_mask lists to t5_mask.npy (uint8)
- Extracts captions to caption.txt and metadata to metadata.json
- --dry-run mode for preview without writing
- Progress reporting with throughput stats
- Skips already-migrated images (idempotent)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Port dataset pipeline from prx-tg as stratum Python package

- src/stratum/ package with CLI entry point (stratum command)
- Per-image directory format (parallel-safe, no central JSONL)
- Pipeline passes: caption, dinov3, t5, pose, pixel (opt-in)
- DWPose ONNX detector (standalone, no mmpose deps)
- Worker sharding (--shard N/M) for multi-GPU parallelism
- Dataset status and verification commands
- HuggingFace publish stub for incremental releases
- Updated copilot-instructions.md for new architecture

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add generate-agent skill (#17)

Pure LLM skill that mints a Copilot CLI agent with a distinct personality.
Asks three questions (character, role, scope), then produces two co-located
files: {name}.agent.md (operating instructions) and {name}.soul.md
(personality and identity).

Supports repo scope (.github/agents/) and user scope (~/.copilot/agents/).
Genesis-agnostic — no working-memory or continuity references.

Co-authored-by: Ian Philpot <ianphil@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(scripts): regenerate package-lock.json via npm instead of sed (#2639)

Fixes the issue where set-project-version.sh incorrectly replaced
library dependency versions in package-lock.json files that happened
to match the current project version.

Changes:
- Remove package-lock.json files from the sed replacement loop
- Exclude node_modules from find commands for package.json
- Add a new step that runs 'npm install --package-lock-only' in each
  directory containing a package-lock.json to correctly regenerate
  the lock file without touching library versions

Co-authored-by: kthoms <265597+kthoms@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Audit and standardize all docstrings and inline comments

- Enforce NumPy docstring style across loki/system.py, loki/config.py,
  loki/cli.py, and tools/kiwix_tool.py
- Expand one-liner docstrings to include Parameters, Returns, Raises,
  and Notes sections where applicable
- Update stale setup() docstring to reflect the 6-step interactive wizard
- Update start() docstring to mention mDNS broadcasting
- Add Parameters and Raises sections to _require_tool()
- Add Returns sections to all path-returning helpers in config.py
  (kiwix_dir, caddyfile_path, env_file_path, loki_root, avahi_pid_file,
  _default_root)
- Expand all system.py function docstrings with full NumPy sections
- Remove numbered step markers from setup() inline comments (Step 0..5)
- Remove trailing periods from all # inline comments
- Remove agentic narration comments from kiwix_tool.py that described
  the 'what' rather than the 'why'; keep the one comment explaining
  the href-prefix filter rationale

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Implement adversarial review refinements

- configure_ollama_binding: capture daemon-reload returncode and return
  False immediately if it fails, rather than silently continuing to restart
- loki start: guard avahi-publish-address spawn behind is_installed() check;
  emit a Warning pointing to 'loki setup' if the binary is absent
- test_system.py: apply autospec=True + spec=subprocess.CompletedProcess
  uniformly to all 12 subprocess.run patches for signature enforcement
- Add test_configure_ollama_binding_returns_false_on_daemon_reload_failure
  using side_effect=[ok, ok, fail] to verify early-return on reload error
- Add test_start_warns_when_avahi_publish_not_installed confirming the new
  is_installed guard fires and avahi spawn is skipped

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: loki

commit.message:
feat: Linux-only automated setup with loki setup, mDNS, and LOKI_ROOT

- Add loki/system.py with all OS-level operations: package manager
  detection (apt-get/dnf), Docker/Ollama installation via official
  scripts, Ollama systemd network binding override, shell profile
  LOKI_ROOT export, and avahi-publish-address process management
  (start with PID file, terminate with liveness check via SIGTERM)

- Update loki setup with 6 interactive steps:
  0. Display config.yaml and confirm before proceeding (N exits)
  1. Install missing packages (aria2, avahi-daemon, avahi-utils)
  2. Install Docker via get.docker.com + usermod -aG docker
  3. Install Ollama via ollama.com/install.sh
  4. Configure Ollama systemd binding (OLLAMA_HOST=0.0.0.0 override)
  5. Add export LOKI_ROOT=<path> to shell profile
  Each step prompts [Y/n]; decline prints 'see README' notice

- Update loki start: spawn avahi-publish-address in background after
  docker compose up for .local URLs; skip with notice otherwise

- Update loki stop: terminate avahi-publish process via .avahi.pid
  before docker compose down

- Add loki_root() and avahi_pid_file() to loki/config.py

- Rewrite README.md for Linux-only: remove all macOS/Windows content
  (Bonjour, dns-sd, brew, avahi XML service file, manual systemd
  override steps); document loki setup as single-command installer
  with manual fallback section

- Add .avahi.pid to .gitignore

- Add 42 new tests across test_setup_cmd.py, test_start_cmd.py,
  test_stop_cmd.py, and new tests/test_system.py; update conftest
  with _stub_system autouse fixture patching at loki.cli.* import site

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: grant contents:write and fix shell logic in api-watch workflow

The api-watch workflow was failing with a 403 on git push because
permissions only granted contents:read. Changed to contents:write.

Also fixed a shell operator precedence bug where:
  git diff --cached --quiet || git commit ... && git push
would always run git push (even with no changes) due to left-to-right
evaluation of || and &&. Replaced with an explicit if block.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: bump version to 3.12.1

Update CHANGELOG.md, AGENTS.md, docs/roadmap.md, docs/contributing.md
with new test counts (2,798 across 112 files) and v3.12.1 release notes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Scope container names with loki- prefix to avoid host conflicts

Explicit container_name values bypass Docker Compose's automatic
project-name scoping, making them globally unique requirements.
Prefixing with 'loki-' prevents clashes with unrelated containers.

Service names (internal Docker DNS) are unchanged, so the Caddyfile
reverse_proxy and kiwix_tool base URL continue to resolve correctly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
⚡ [performance improvement] optimize planetary data extraction (#72)

* ⚡ [performance improvement] optimize planetary data extraction

Optimized `extract_refined_data.py` by:
1. Combining two separate `for row in sheet` loops into a single pass.
2. Replacing repetitive list comprehensions with a single set creation per row for $O(1)$ value lookups.

Measured improvement: ~49% speed boost on simulated data.
Verified correctness: Results match the original implementation exactly.

fix: サマリーAPIのカテゴリ別集計を実装 (#14)

ExpenseService#getSummaryのカテゴリ集計ロジックを修正。
- expenses リストを Expense#getCategory() でグループ化
- カテゴリごとに合計金額を算出
- percentage は (カテゴリ合計 / 全体合計) * 100 で計算（小数点1位まで）
- total が 0 の場合は percentage を 0.0 にする
- 「未分類」固定のコメントと実装を削除

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Feat/emacs pkg (#4)

* feat: add summary DTOs and QuerySummaries for lightweight log listing

- Create internal/management/dto.go with LogSummary, LogListResponse,
  LogDetailResponse, LogDetailEntry, StreamViewResponse, BodyResponse,
  and APIError types
- Add database.LogSummary struct to models.go for DB scan targets
- Add LogReader.QuerySummaries() method selecting only summary columns
  (no bodies/headers) with computed body-length fields
- Update apiLogsHandler to use QuerySummaries and return LogListResponse
  envelope with pagination metadata (limit, offset, has_more)
- Update apiLogByIDHandler to return LogDetailResponse with decoded
  headers and no body fields
- Add writeAPIError, decodeHeaders, derefStr helpers
- Add ManagementConfig.PreviewBytes (default 65536) with validation
- Update example-config.yaml with preview_bytes setting
- Remove old logDetailResponse/streamViewResponse types from api.go

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add FTS5 full-text search indexing for openai_logs

- Add ftsSchema constant with FTS5 virtual table and sync triggers
- Add migrateFTS() with automatic rebuild for existing data
- Update buildWhere() to use FTS5 MATCH instead of LIKE
- Add FTS5 search test with distinct body content
- Enable sqlite_fts5 build tag in build script and Dockerfile

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Wire query parser into list API and add body retrieval endpoint

- Add query DSL support to apiLogsHandler via the 'query' parameter,
  using internal/query.Parse and query.ToSQL. Old individual params
  (status, path, since, until, q) remain as backward-compatible fallback.
- Add QuerySummariesRaw and CountRaw methods to LogReader that accept
  extra WHERE clause and args for the parsed query DSL.
- Add GET /api/logs/{id}/body endpoint with part (req/resp), mode
  (raw/assembled), and full parameters. Uses streamview.Build for
  assembled mode.
- Refactor apiLogByIDHandler into apiLogSubHandler that dispatches
  between detail and body sub-routes.
- Add PreviewBytes field to ServerDeps, wired from config.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update web UI for new management API endpoint split

- Replace three separate filter inputs (status, path, search) with a
  unified query DSL input that sends a 'query' parameter
- Update detail modal to use new API shape: headers are now parsed
  objects (req_headers/resp_headers) instead of JSON strings
- Add on-demand body loading via GET /api/logs/{id}/body endpoint
  with Load Request/Response Body buttons and Load Full for truncated
- Stream view assembled toggle now fetches from body endpoint
- Show included_bytes / total_bytes info from body responses
- Update index.html to match new single query filter input

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add comprehensive tests for body endpoint, query DSL, and summary shape

- Body endpoint tests (10): raw req/resp, assembled SSE, invalid
  combination, preview truncation, full mode, not found, empty body,
  default params, invalid part
- Query DSL wiring tests (2): filter by status code, parse error
- Summary list shape test (1): verify has_request_body/has_response_body
  present and body/header fields absent

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: emacs pkg: memoryelaine (and adptions)

  Implementation Complete — All 21 Tasks Done

  Phase 1: Go Server (11 tasks)

   - API redesign: Summary/detail/body endpoint split — list returns lightweight summaries, detail returns metadata+headers (no bodies), body endpoint serves content on demand
   - FTS5 indexing: Full-text search on request/response bodies with sync triggers and auto-migration
   - Query DSL parser: status:200 method:POST path:/chat "exact phrase" with parameterized SQL (no injection)
   - Web UI + TUI: Updated to use new API (query DSL input, on-demand body loading)
   - 28 server tests (14 new), updated docs and design spec

  Phase 2: Emacs Package (10 tasks)

  8 modules in /work/emacs/:

   - memoryelaine.el — entry point, defcustoms, autoloads
   - memoryelaine-auth.el — auth-source → explicit → interactive prompt
   - memoryelaine-http.el — async curl with generation counters, stale response suppression
   - memoryelaine-state.el — global search + buffer-local detail state
   - memoryelaine-search.el — tabulated-list-mode with query DSL, pagination, recording toggle, live search
   - memoryelaine-show.el — detail buffer with on-demand body loading, stream view toggle
   - memoryelaine-log.el — timestamped diagnostic buffer
   - memoryelaine-test.el — 25 ERT tests, all passing

  Test Results

   - 143 Go tests passing | 25 Emacs ERT tests passing

* Emacs build script, code review, and second opinion

* docs: fix mismatches between spec/README and Go implementation

- Fix DSL flag names: is:truncated -> is:req-truncated/is:resp-truncated,
  has:req-body/has:resp-body -> has:req/has:resp (matches parser.go)
- Fix assembled mode description: returns available:false with reason,
  does not fall back to raw (matches api.go)
- Add limit, offset, has_more to /api/logs response example (matches dto.go)
- Add Emacs client to list of consumer surfaces

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
